[805]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 弟夫妇反共劝慰 - My brother and his wife came to console her; '反共' means 'came back' or 'returned'.
  • 固知嫂无他意 - We know you have no other intentions; '固' emphasizes certainty.
  • 此必别有故 - There must be some other reason; '别有故' indicates a hidden cause.
  • 吾夫妇年尚少 - My husband and I are still young; '尚' indicates relative youth.
  • 儿死可再生 - Our son can be reborn; a metaphorical expression suggesting hope.
  • 愿嫂无介意 - We hope you won't be upset; '介意' means to take offense.
  • 妇乃出所制衣履 - The woman then took out the clothes and shoes she had made; '乃' indicates a sequence of actions.
  • 为儿著之 - Dressed them on him; '著' means 'to wear'.
  • 且买良材为作椟 - And bought good materials to make a coffin; '椟' refers to a coffin.
  • 既敛 - After the burial; '敛' means 'to bury'.
  • 妇百计求死 - The woman sought death in every way; '百计' emphasizes her desperation.
  • 弟夫妇防之甚严 - My brother and I guarded her very strictly; '甚严' indicates extreme vigilance.
  • 城外故有田宅 - There was land and property outside the city; '故有' means 'previously owned'.
  • 妇托故至城外 - The woman pretended to go to the countryside; '托故' means to make an excuse.
  • 弟夫妇亦从之往 - My brother and I followed her; '从之' means 'followed her'.
  • 妇乃绝食 - The woman then stopped eating; '绝食' means 'to fast to death'.
  • 吾二人亦不食 - We will also not eat; '亦' indicates mutual action.
  • 吾杀此儿 - I killed this child; a direct statement of the woman's action.
  • 何面目见尔夫妇 - How can I face you both; '何面目' means 'how can I have the face to'.
  • 且亦何以见吾夫哉 - And how can I face my husband; '何以' means 'how can I'.
  • 吾夫妇老矣，不能生子 - My husband and I are old and cannot have more children; '老矣' emphasizes age.
  • 万一尔夫妇竟无子 - If you two never have a child; '万一' means 'if only'.
  • 杨氏血食不以吾斩乎 - Will the Yang family's sacrifices be cut off because of me; '血食' refers to ancestral sacrifices.
  • 此妇之杀此儿，必有厉鬼凭依之 - This woman's killing of the child must have been influenced by a malevolent spirit; '厉鬼凭依' means 'possessed by a malevolent spirit'.
  • 袁子才先生《新齐谐》载有舅杀其甥事，与此类也 - Mr. Yuan Zicai's *New Qi Xie* records a case of an uncle killing his nephew, similar to this; '载有' means 'records' and '与此类也' means 'similar to this'.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 弟夫妇 - Refers to the narrator's brother and his wife, who are involved in the story.
  • 妇 - Refers to the narrator's sister-in-law, who is the central figure in the story.
  • 城外 - Refers to the countryside outside the city, a common setting for such stories.
  • 袁子才先生《新齐谐》 - Refers to Yuan Zicai's *New Qi Xie*, a collection of strange tales and supernatural stories.
  • 厉鬼凭依 - Refers to the belief in malevolent spirits possessing people, a common theme in Chinese folklore and literature.

Proper Nouns

  • 袁子才先生 - Yuan Zicai, a historical figure known for his writings.
  • 《新齐谐》 - *New Qi Xie*, a collection of strange tales and supernatural stories.

[806]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 贼陷苏州 - Refers to the rebels occupying Suzhou, where '贼' refers to the Taiping rebels.
  • 得人家所弃女子 - Means 'found a girl abandoned by a family.'
  • 遂收养之 - Means 'and took her in to raise.'
  • 年逾四十 - Means 'over forty years old.'
  • 学贾于外 - Means 'studying trade outside.'
  • 携其二幼女 - Means 'taking her two young daughters.'
  • 视其长女 - Means 'to visit her eldest daughter.'
  • 杳无踪迹 - Means 'completely without trace.'
  • 判归朱 - Means 'the court ruled in favor of Zhu.'
  • 郁郁不乐 - Means 'depressed and unhappy.'
  • 道路往返耗其资 - Means 'the back-and-forth travel depleted his resources.'
  • 恒至余家助爨烹之役 - Means 'he would often come to our house to help with cooking and other chores.'
  • 婢媪辈言其事如此 - Means 'the maids and female servants spoke of the matter in this way.'
  • 老夫女妻过以相与 - Means 'the old man and his young wife lived together.'
  • 然既生育子女，且皆成立 - Means 'but since they had already raised children, and all had grown up.'
  • 乃舍之而去，遂同陌路 - Means 'she left him and went her own way, becoming strangers.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 咸丰庚申年 - Refers to the year 1860, during the Taiping Rebellion.
  • 贼 - Refers to the Taiping rebels, a major rebel group during the mid-19th century.
  • 苏州 - Refers to Suzhou, a city in Jiangsu Province, China.
  • 临顿路 - Refers to a street in Suzhou.
  • 湖州 - Refers to Huzhou, another city in Zhejiang Province, China.
  • 金匮县 - Refers to a county, likely in Jiangsu Province, where Zhu sought help.

Proper Nouns

  • 朱某 - Refers to Zhu, the main character of the story.
  • 苏州 - Refers to Suzhou, a city in Jiangsu Province, China.
  • 临顿路 - Refers to a street in Suzhou.
  • 湖州 - Refers to Huzhou, another city in Zhejiang Province, China.
  • 金匮县 - Refers to a county, likely in Jiangsu Province, where Zhu sought help.

[807]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 輕薄桃花逐水流 - Metaphor from Du Fu's poem, meaning 'light and frivolous like peach blossoms that drift with the current,' used to describe a carefree or fickle woman.
  • 庚申之亂 - Refers to the chaos or upheaval of the year 1660 (the year of the Gengshen cycle in the Chinese zodiac), likely referring to the Qing dynasty's consolidation of power in China.
  • 負其一子一女 - Literally 'carrying her son and daughter,' but implies the strain of carrying two children.
  • 已而惫甚 - Means 'soon afterward, she was extremely exhausted,' with '已而' indicating a short time after an event.
  • 度不能兼全 - Means 'she judged that she could not save both,' with '度' indicating a judgment or estimation.
  • 擬棄其女 - Means 'she planned to abandon her daughter,' with '擬' indicating a plan or intention.
  • 牽衣不釋 - Means 'she clung to her mother's clothes,' with '不釋' indicating refusal to let go.
  • 置之地 - Means 'she placed her on the ground,' with '置' indicating placement.
  • 疾負其子度橋而去 - Means 'she quickly carried her son across the bridge and left,' with '疾' indicating speed and '去' indicating departure.
  • 武 - A unit of distance, equivalent to a step or pace.
  • 索陪門財 - Means 'demanded dowry money,' with '索' indicating demand and '陪門財' referring to the dowry.
  • 衣裙钗钏 - Refers to clothes, skirts, hairpins, and bracelets, collectively indicating valuable possessions.
  • 不順其姑 - Means 'did not obey her mother-in-law,' with '姑' referring to the mother-in-law.
  • 時有谇语 - Means 'frequently had quarrels,' with '谇语' referring to arguments or disputes.
  • 積佣值 - Means 'accumulated wages,' with '積' indicating accumulation and '佣值' referring to wages.
  • 一朝而罄 - Means 'all spent in one go,' with '罄' indicating exhaustion or depletion.
  • 轉世 - Refers to rebirth or reincarnation, with '轉世' indicating the concept of being reborn in a new life.
  • 臆度之辞 - Means 'speculative statements,' with '臆度' indicating speculation and '辭' referring to words or statements.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 杜老 - Refers to Du Fu (杜甫), a renowned Tang dynasty poet.
  • 庚申之亂 - Likely refers to the chaos of the early Qing dynasty, specifically the consolidation of power in 1660.
  • 傭媪朱氏 - Refers to an elderly woman named Zhu, who was a servant in the author's household.
  • 陪門財 - Refers to the traditional Chinese dowry system, where the bride's family provides financial support to the groom's family.
  • 姑 - Refers to the mother-in-law, a key figure in traditional Chinese family dynamics.
  • 轉世 - Refers to the Buddhist concept of rebirth or reincarnation, which was influential in Chinese culture.

Proper Nouns

  • 杜老 - Du Fu, a famous Tang dynasty poet.
  • 傭媪朱氏 - Zhu, an elderly woman who was a servant in the author's household.
  • 庚申 - The year 1660, during the early Qing dynasty.

[808]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 逃至上海 - Literal meaning 'fled to Shanghai,' used to describe Se's escape from the turmoil.
  • 颇有口辩 - Means 'has a good command of speech' or 'is eloquent.'
  • 延之至其寓 - Means 'invited him to their residence.'
  • 使佐会计 - Means 'made him assist with accounting.'
  • 大有所获 - Means 'made a lot of money.'
  • 艳之 - Means 'found him attractive.'
  • 欲娶为妻 - Means 'wanted to marry him.'
  • 愿委身 - Means 'willing to submit herself.'
  • 故有夫在 - Means 'since she already had a husband.'
  • 计无所出 - Means 'had no solution.'
  • 素以姊称之 - Means 'always called her elder sister.'
  • 伪欲如某处 - Means 'pretended to go to a certain place.'
  • 突挤舟人坠江中 - Means 'suddenly pushed the boatman into the river.'
  • 反号呼拯救 - Means 'then shouted for help.'
  • 遂瞑眩而仆 - Means 'then fell into a faint.'
  • 仆地遂绝 - Means 'fell to the ground and died.'
  • 仍居沪上 - Means 'still lived in Shanghai.'
  • 于妇死前数日 - Means 'several days before the woman's death.'
  • 若与人争辩者 - Means 'as if he were arguing with someone.'
  • 从尔去 - Means 'follow you.'
  • 赴水而死 - Means 'drowned himself.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 苏州 - Refers to Suzhou, a city in Jiangsu Province, known for its rich cultural heritage.
  • 上海 - Refers to Shanghai, a major city in eastern China, known for its international trade and commerce.
  • 泰西人 - Refers to Westerners, particularly those from Europe.
  • 沪渎 - Refers to the Huangpu River, which flows through Shanghai.
  • 黄浦 - Refers to the Huangpu River, where the boatman's wife's husband was killed.
  • 乱定 - Refers to the end of a period of chaos or turmoil.

Proper Nouns

  • 沈某 - Refers to Se, the main character.
  • 长子 - Refers to the boatman's wife's friend, called 'Elder Sister.'

[809]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 當兵亂時 - Phrase indicating the time of military chaos or war.
  • 隨其母避亂至上海 - Means 'followed his mother to escape the chaos to Shanghai.'
  • 门前大树上有红衣人招我 - Literal description of a red-clothed figure waving at the narrator from a tree.
  • 勿妄言，目眩耳 - Mother's rebuke, meaning 'don't speak nonsense, you're hallucinating.'
  • 攀援登树 - Means 'climbed up the tree.'
  • 坐义桠中 - Colloquial term for sitting in a fork of a tree branch.
  • 俄枝断，坠地死 - Means 'suddenly the branch broke, and he fell to the ground and died.'
  • 大儿妇因言 - Means 'the eldest son's wife then said.'
  • 饭后不饮茶，所食不化 - Common saying, meaning 'after a meal, don't drink tea, as it prevents digestion.'
  • 汝焉往？且饮茶去 - Means 'Where are you going? Go drink some tea first.'
  • 门外有人呼我，我出即返耳 - Means 'There's someone calling me outside; I'll just go out and come back right away.'
  • 投水而死 - Means 'threw himself into the water and died.'
  • 殆所谓夙冤者与 - Speculative phrase meaning 'perhaps this is what is meant by a long-standing grudge or karmic debt?'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 苏州 - The city of Suzhou, known for its rich history and cultural heritage.
  • 上海 - The city of Shanghai, a major port city and commercial hub.
  • 红衣人 - A figure dressed in red, possibly symbolic or supernatural.
  • 夙冤 - A term referring to a long-standing grudge or karmic debt, often associated with Buddhist or Taoist beliefs.

Proper Nouns

  • 苏州 - The city of Suzhou.
  • 上海 - The city of Shanghai.
  • 陈氏子 - The son of the Chen family.
  • 余氏女 - The daughter of the Yu family.

[810]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 湖北咸寧 - Refers to Xianning, Hubei Province, a specific geographical location.
  • 余氏女 - Refers to a woman from the Yu family.
  • 嫁為石氏婦 - Means 'married into the Shi family.'
  • 石故無賴 - Describes Shi as a good-for-nothing or a scoundrel.
  • 日事飲博 - Means 'daily engaged in drinking and gambling.'
  • 余女生一子而卒 - Indicates that Yu's daughter gave birth to a son and then died.
  • 余氏收而蓄之 - Means 'the Yu family took him in and raised him.'
  • 使出而學賈 - Means 'sent him out to learn trade.'
  • 其子颇勤谨 - Describes the son as diligent and careful.
  • 師甚爱之 - Means 'the teacher loved him very much.'
  • 垂三年矣 - Means 'for nearly three years.'
  • 石闻其子稍有所蓄 - Means 'Shi heard that his son had accumulated some wealth.'
  • 余氏所具衣装冬夏皆备 - Means 'the Yu family had all the clothes for winter and summer ready.'
  • 乃自诣其师 - Means 'then went to see his teacher.'
  • 欲以其子归 - Means 'wanted to take his son back.'
  • 师留之 - Means 'the teacher tried to keep him.'
  • 石不可 - Means 'Shi refused.'
  • 吾子与汝为徒耳，非与汝为奴也，汝安得留 - Means 'My son is your apprentice, not your slave; how can you keep him?'
  • 师知不可夺 - Means 'the teacher knew he could not change Shi's mind.'
  • 厚赠而遣之 - Means 'gave him generous gifts and let him go.'
  • 其地距石所居三四百里 - Means 'the place was about three to four hundred miles away from where Shi lived.'
  • 石挈其子至半途 - Means 'Shi took his son to the halfway point.'
  • 适阻小水 - Means 'just happened to be blocked by a small stream.'
  • 乃赁一舟尽载所赍 - Means 'then rented a boat to carry everything he had.'
  • 父子俱登舟矣 - Means 'both father and son got on the boat.'
  • 石语其子曰 - Means 'Shi told his son.'
  • 吾遗一物在某处，可往取之 - Means 'I left something at a certain place; you can go get it.'
  • 子登岸至其地 - Means 'the son went ashore to the place.'
  • 遍觅无有 - Means 'searched everywhere but found nothing.'
  • 将返问之父 - Means 'was about to return to ask his father.'
  • 比至水滨 - Means 'by the time he reached the water's edge.'
  • 则舟已开矣 - Means 'the boat had already set sail.'
  • 沿岸追寻 - Means 'tried to follow along the shore.'
  • 无所睹 - Means 'saw nothing.'
  • 乃大窘 - Means 'was greatly distressed.'
  • 亻累然一身 - Means 'alone and helpless.'
  • 进退俱不可 - Means 'neither advancing nor retreating was possible.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 湖北咸宁 - Xianning, Hubei Province, a region in China.
  • 余氏 - A family name, indicating the family of Yu.
  • 石氏 - A family name, indicating the family of Shi.
  • 无赖 - A term often used to describe someone who is a good-for-nothing or a scoundrel.
  • 饮博 - Refers to drinking and gambling, common vices in traditional Chinese society.
  • 学贾 - Learning trade or business, a practical skill in ancient China.
  • 勤谨 - Diligent and careful, positive character traits valued in traditional Chinese culture.
  • 师 - Teacher, a respected figure in traditional Chinese education.
  • 徒 - Apprentice, a person learning a trade or skill under a master.

Proper Nouns

  • 湖北咸宁 - Xianning, Hubei Province.
  • 余氏 - The Yu family.
  • 石氏 - The Shi family.
  • 无赖 - A term used to describe a good-for-nothing or a scoundrel.
  • 饮博 - Drinking and gambling.
  • 学贾 - Learning trade or business.
  • 勤谨 - Diligent and careful.
  • 师 - Teacher.
  • 徒 - Apprentice.

[811]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 不得已 - Literally 'not having any choice,' meaning 'unavoidably' or 'out of necessity.'
  • 乞食于途 - 'To beg for food on the road,' indicating extreme poverty and wandering.
  • 跖穿膝暴 - Compound expression describing extreme physical hardship: 'soles of the feet worn through, knees exposed and raw.'
  • 胫骨腐烂生且 - Rare and difficult character 且 (possibly a variant of 疽 *jū*, meaning abscess or ulcer) appears here. The phrase likely means 'the shins' bones rot and ulcers form.'
  • 病不能行 - 'Sick and unable to walk,' indicating physical incapacitation.
  • 群モ怜而饮食之 - The character モ is likely a typo or misrendered character. Based on context, it may be 民 *mín* (people) or 众 *zhòng* (crowd). The phrase means 'the people (or crowd) pitied him and gave him food and drink.'
  • 得不死 - 'Managed to survive' or 'did not die.'
  • 憔悴无人状矣 - 'Looks emaciated and inhuman,' describing his physical and emotional state.
  • 持养数月 - 'Nursed and cared for for several months.'
  • 乃始能行 - 'Only then could he walk again.'
  • 使学贾于他处 - 'Sent him to study commerce elsewhere.' 贾 *gǔ* refers to trade or commerce.
  • 戒之曰 - 'Warned him, saying...' a formal way of giving instruction.
  • 汝父若来，勿再为所诳也 - 'If your father comes, do not be deceived by him again.' 诳 *kuáng* means 'to deceive' or 'to trick.'
  • 杀越人于货 - 'Killing a man from Yue for his goods.' 越人 *yuè rén* refers to people from the ancient state of Yue, often used metaphorically for strangers or outsiders.
  • 尚且有之 - 'There are still such cases,' indicating that such behavior is not uncommon.
  • 况止于欺诳 - 'How much less so is it when it is only deception.' 况 *huàng* is used in a comparative structure.
  • 又何足异 - 'How strange is that?' or 'What is so unusual about it?'
  • 所异者 - 'What is unusual is...'
  • 以父诳其子而取其资 - 'The father deceives his son and takes his money.' 资 *zī* means 'money' or 'resources.'
  • 是不可以人理论矣 - 'This cannot be explained by human reason or morality.' 人理 *rén lǐ* refers to human reason or moral order.
  • 余书为搜神志怪而作 - 'My book is written to record strange and supernatural occurrences.' 搜神志怪 *sōu shén zhì guài* is a genre of classical Chinese supernatural literature.
  • 人无人理，人即妖也 - 'If a person lacks human reason, then that person is a demon.' 妖 *yāo* means 'demon' or 'supernatural being.'
  • 正不必魑魅罔两，然后可铸之禹鼎也 - 'One does not even need to be a *chī mèi wǎng liǎng* (spirits or demons) to be cast into the *Yú Dǐng* (the great bronze cauldron used to punish criminals).' 禹鼎 *Yú Dǐng* is a legendary cauldron from the time of the great sage Yu, symbolizing divine justice.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 咸宁 - A historical place name, likely referring to Xianning, an ancient county in present-day Hubei or Guangdong, depending on the context.
  • 余氏 - A family name, 'Yu’s family.'
  • 搜神志怪 - A classical Chinese genre of supernatural tales, often collected in works like *Shi Shuo Xin Yu* (A New Account of Tales of the World) or *Shen Hui Zhi Guai* (Record of Strange Supernatural Events).
  • 禹鼎 - The legendary bronze cauldron from the time of Yu the Great, used symbolically in ancient texts to represent divine punishment for moral transgressors.
  • 魑魅罔两 - A set of mythical spirits or demons: *chī mèi* are forest spirits, and *wǎng liǎng* are spirits that haunt people. These are commonly referenced in classical Chinese texts as supernatural beings.
  • 杀越人于货 - Refers to the crime of robbery or murder for profit, a common theme in classical moral and legal discourse.

Proper Nouns

  • 咸宁 - A place name, likely Xianning.
  • 余氏 - A family name, 'Yu’s family.'
  • 禹鼎 - A legendary bronze cauldron from the time of Yu the Great.

[812]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 咸寧余氏 - Refers to the Yu family of the Xining period, indicating the family's location and time period.
  • 舅姑 - Refers to the husband's parents, specifically the father-in-law and mother-in-law.
  • 續娶 - Means 'to remarry' or 'to take a new wife,' indicating the husband's potential remarriage after the wife's death.
  • 雁之費 - Refers to the expenses associated with a formal marriage ceremony, including the 'dove' (a symbol of the bride) and 'goose' (a symbol of the groom).
  • 嬲不休 - Means 'to pester endlessly,' describing the persistent behavior of family members.
  • 無與父母家矣 - Means 'no longer have any connection with the parents' family,' indicating the wife's severance from her natal family after marriage.
  • 續娶得子 - Means 'to remarry and have a son,' indicating the husband's potential remarriage and having a child.
  • 所娶者裝送颇盛 - Means 'the new wife brought a substantial dowry,' indicating the wealth of the new wife's family.
  • 嗣族人子为子 - Means 'to adopt a son from the clan,' indicating the practice of adopting a son from the extended family.
  • 抱孙矣 - Means 'has a grandson,' indicating the continuation of the family line through the adopted son.
  • 足慰此妇于地下乎 - Means 'is it enough to comfort this woman in the afterlife,' expressing a sense of closure or satisfaction for the deceased wife.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 咸寧 - Refers to the Xining period (275-289 AD) during the Western Jin Dynasty, indicating the time period of the story.
  • 余氏 - Refers to the Yu family, indicating the family's surname and lineage.
  • 夫家 - Refers to the husband's family, indicating the wife's marital family.
  • 父母家 - Refers to the wife's natal family, indicating her family of origin.
  • 雁之费 - Refers to the traditional Chinese wedding customs, including the symbols of the dove and goose, which were used in the wedding ceremony.
  • 嬲不休 - Refers to the persistent behavior of family members, particularly the wife's family, who might visit the husband's family after her death.
  • 续娶 - Refers to the practice of remarriage in traditional Chinese society, where a husband might remarry if his first wife died without children.
  • 嗣族人子为子 - Refers to the practice of adopting a son from the extended family, which was a common practice in traditional Chinese society to continue the family line.

Proper Nouns

  • 咸寧 - The Xining period (275-289 AD) during the Western Jin Dynasty.
  • 余氏 - The Yu family.
  • 舅姑 - The husband's parents (father-in-law and mother-in-law).
  • 续娶 - Remarriage.
  • 雁之费 - Expenses associated with a formal marriage ceremony.
  • 嬲不休 - Pester endlessly.
  • 无与父母家矣 - No longer have any connection with the parents' family.
  • 续娶得子 - Remarry and have a son.
  • 所娶者装送颇盛 - The new wife brought a substantial dowry.
  • 嗣族人子为子 - Adopt a son from the clan.
  • 抱孙矣 - Has a grandson.
  • 足慰此妇于地下乎 - Is it enough to comfort this woman in the afterlife.

[813]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 胎教之法 - Refers to the ancient practice of controlling the environment and behavior of pregnant women to influence the fetus.
  • 避忌之说 - Refers to superstitious beliefs about avoidance, aimed at preventing harm or misfortune.
  • 换胎 - A superstitious belief that what a pregnant woman sees can change the nature of her unborn child.
  • 屈右臂，伸三指，作指物状 - Describes a specific gesture where the right arm is bent, and three fingers are extended, as if pointing at something.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 湖北 - Hubei Province, a region in central China, where specific superstitious practices were observed.

Proper Nouns

  • 湖北 - Hubei Province, a region in central China.

[814]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 偶观 - Happened to watch or accidentally saw, indicating an unplanned encounter.
  • 换胎 - Mythical or supernatural change in the fetus.
  • 相持 - Hold this position, describing a state of mutual resistance or tension.
